title: Wicked Malted Uruguayan Cheese Stir-fry
description: A midnight-craving, malty uruguayan dish that delivers a cheese-pull experience with a hint of mirin-hon, sautéed in a kadhai for a Mediterranean-friendly delight.
instructions: Heat the kadhai over medium heat and drizzle in the olive oil, letting it shimmer.
Add the sliced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until the onion becomes translucent and fragrant.
Introduce the red bell pepper slices and broccoli florets, stirring frequently for 4 minutes until vegetables start to soften.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika over the vegetables and mix well, letting the spice bloom for 30 seconds.
Fold in the shredded Malted Uruguayan cheddar cheese, allowing it to melt and mingle with the veggies.
Pour the mirin-hon over the cheese mixture, stirring to create a glossy, slightly sweet coating.
Stir in the toasted pine nuts, grated Parmesan, and lemon zest, cooking for an additional 2 minutes to further toast the nuts.
Gently fold in the cooked quinoa, ensuring the grains are heated through and coated with sauce.
Remove the kadhai from heat and let the dish sit for 1 minute to meld the flavors.
Serve hot, garnishing with optional fresh parsley, and enjoy the midnight-craving cheese-pull experience.
